_comment_sysupgrade: Path to OpenIPC's sysupgrade. flashd invokes it as 'sysupgrade --archive=<image>' (adding -n on wipe_overlay, --force_all on a force flash) — no wrapper needed. Override only if sysupgrade lives elsewhere on your build.
_comment_staging: staging_dir is tmpfs (RAM) on OpenIPC — the only reliably-writable scratch. Keep max_image_kb below its free space; the download is capped to it so tmpfs cannot be filled.
_comment_require_sha256: default true. Set false to allow apply without a sha256 (relies on the flash tool's own validation, e.g. OpenIPC sysupgrade .md5sum checks) — for mirrors of a trusted upstream. A supplied sha256 is always verified.
_comment_wipe_overlay: default true. Pass -n/--wipe_overlay to sysupgrade so the config overlay (mtd 'rootfs_data') is erased on flash and no stale data survives the upgrade. Set false to preserve /etc across a flash; an apply request may override per-flash with its own "wipe_overlay".
_comment_board_override: REQUIRED on stock OpenIPC: os-release usually has no BOARD= so detection falls back to ID=buildroot and no image matches the board gate. Verify on the device with: cat /etc/openipc-release /etc/os-release; if BOARD=/VERSION are real you may leave this empty, else set the real board id (e.g. ssc338q).
_comment_flash_override: nor|nand; empty = auto-detect from /sys/class/mtd/mtd0/type (standard MTD sysfs, normally reliable). Gates nand images off nor devices (and v.v.). Set only if detection is wrong/absent.
_comment_sources: Firmware sources are declared in sources_file (see configs/sources.json). cache_ttl_s is how long a fetched manifest is reused before re-fetching.
